deductions.reason: Across all three transcripts, the tutor never explains why it is guiding rather than giving answers—no rationale about active reasoning, retention, or skill development is offered. The tutor enforces a Socratic approach consistently but never articulates to the student why this method supports learning.

In Transcript 1, the student makes meaningful progress (correctly distinguishing stressor vs. vulnerability in Turn 7) but the tutor moves straight to the next task without prompting reflection on what changed in the student's thinking. Similarly in Transcripts 2 and 3, when students arrive at stronger reasoning after earlier confusion, the tutor advances without asking the student to reflect on what made their revised approach better.

grade.overview: Across all three transcripts the tutor demonstrates strong Socratic discipline, never giving submission-ready answers, consistently redirecting students to their own reasoning, and building on student progress effectively. The main weaknesses are the absence of any explicit rationale for the tutoring approach (why guided discovery rather than direct answers) and missed opportunities to prompt reflection after students improve. All other criteria are well-met: no double-counting of deductions, no off-topic drift, appropriate tone, and bite-sized responses throughout.
grade.judge_reasoning: 1.1 is full marks: the tutor never produces near-submission-ready content, never rewrites student text, and consistently asks students to generate key ideas before confirming them. 1.2 is full marks: the tutor builds on correct student steps (e.g., affirming the stressor/vulnerability distinction in T1-T7, the chronic vs. episodic insight in T3-T3) and diagnoses confusion before responding. 1.3 loses 2 points under 1.3.A.a because no tutoring rationale is ever offered across any of the three conversations, and 2 points under 1.3.C.a because after students make clear improvements (e.g., T1 Turn 7, T2 Turn 7, T3 Turn 9) the tutor moves forward without prompting reflection on what changed. 1.3.B is not penalized because the tutor does give method-level feedback (e.g., 'don't add health just because it sounds plausible,' 'stop asking is this bad, ask relative to other stressors'). Dialogue quality and communication quality are both full marks: no spiraling, no off-topic drift, responses are appropriately concise and interactive, and tone is consistently supportive without implying formal grading.
